title: Cleome stricta
taxonomy: Cleome stricta is classified in the following way: it belongs to the family Cleomaceae and the order Brassicales. This plant is native to North America and is also known as the Stiff-hairy spiderflower or the Stiff hairy spider flower. It is further classified as a perennial herb, with the species being recognized as a distinct unit within the Cleomaceae family. The classification is also reflected in the plant's botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system. Cleome stricta's classification is used in botanical and horticultural contexts to identify and distinguish the species from other related plants.

intro: Cleome stricta, also known as the prickle poppy or snake flower, is a plant species in the Cleomaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome stricta include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
